purpose: Produce every declared visual slot through a reviewable draft, inspection, cutout, targeted repair, style normalization, acceptance, and binding-ready revision chain while preserving one shared Style DNA and one provenance truth.

forbidden: persist project truth in ComfyUI history
accept a draft or mask as final
use a detached overlay for world roles

requirements: one accepted master identity before pose variants
pose variants derive from the accepted master revision
tile edge or seam checks when repeatable
separate declared depth layers when parallax is required
no generated text unless explicitly declared

draft-generate.rule: Generate one subject or one coherent same-role scene for the work item. A draft is never binding-ready.

vision-inspect.rule: Return factual evidence including detected subjects, semantic match, bbox or mask suggestion, artifacts, background and confidence; never return acceptance.

subject-segment.rule: Use a pinned segmentation workflow such as detector plus SAM or an approved matting workflow. The mask is versioned and independently validated.

masked-repair.rule: Repair only declared failed regions. Every edit creates a child revision and returns to inspection.

color-plan.rule: Select declared palette roles and protected regions. Never copy a free-form palette into the workflow or infer color from a screenshot alone.

color-apply.rule: Use deterministic recolor when region masks and palette mapping are sufficient; use a pinned image-edit workflow only when new shaded pixels are required. Every color result returns to inspection.

style-normalize.rule: Apply only deterministic alpha cleanup, dimensions, outline, shadow, palette limits, trim and anchor operations declared by the Style DNA and family policy.

final-review.rule: The AssetAcceptanceGate alone decides accepted, repairable, rejected or debt.

SameFamilyAssemblyReceipt.rule: Optional deterministic packaging after source acceptance; it is not a generation or acceptance shortcut.

canonicalFlow: validate AssetProductionRequest against the pinned template and visual slot declarations
compile one immutable AssetProductionSetPlan with one work item per required slot
resolve reusable accepted revisions before requesting new pixels
execute each work item through its declared role-specific recipe on one governed ComfyUI service queue
persist every draft, mask, repair and normalized result as an immutable revision or receipt
loop only the failed work item or masked region within its retry budget
accept every required work item independently
emit AssetProductionSetAcceptanceReceipt only when required target visual slot coverage is complete
optionally assemble accepted same-family assets or frames through LocalDerivationKernel
pass accepted final revisions to RuntimeAssetBinder by targetVisualSlotId

playableVersionGate.rule: A newly created or continued project version is not playable, committable, or selectable until every required work item is accepted, every required targetVisualSlotId has a valid binding receipt, and aggregate runtime validation passes.
playableVersionGate.whileBuilding: The previous immutable playable version may remain available, but no draft or partially completed new version may be presented as playable.
playableVersionGate.mutationModel: A user change starts a new production set derived from the active immutable ProjectVersion. It never hot-swaps an intermediate asset into the running game.
playableVersionGate.commitModel: After full asset coverage, binding and runtime validation, ProjectStore atomically commits a new immutable ProjectVersion and advances the active version pointer.
playableVersionGate.failureModel: If any required asset ends rejected or debt, the new version is not committed and the previous active version remains unchanged.
forbidden: runtime hot swap of draft or candidate revisions
partially generated game presented as playable
preview binding used as release evidence
active ProjectVersion changed before full aggregate acceptance

iteration: OBSERVE: run vision inspection and deterministic pixel checks against the current immutable revision
DIAGNOSE: compile typed defects and satisfied checks without mutating pixels
PLAN: choose exactly one next action and reserve its retry, time and provider budget
ACT: execute generation, segmentation, masked edit, color application or deterministic normalization
REVISE: persist the output as a child revision with all execution receipts
REOBSERVE: every pixel-changing action returns to OBSERVE; previous evidence cannot certify new pixels
DECIDE: accept only when all required checks pass, otherwise continue within budget or end in explicit rejected/debt
decisionPriority: invalid provider output or corrupt pixels -> rejected/debt
wrong subject or unrecoverable composition -> regenerate draft within generation budget
multiple subjects or background contamination -> segment and cut out
localized anatomy, edge or artifact defect -> masked repair
palette or color-role mismatch -> color plan then deterministic recolor or masked image edit
outline, alpha, size, anchor or simple shadow mismatch -> deterministic style normalization
all required checks satisfied -> final review
budget exhausted -> explicit debt with recovery phase and owner
invariants: one action plan selects exactly one primary pixel-changing action
every pixel-changing output receives a new revision id and sha256
every pixel-changing output invalidates prior visual and deterministic acceptance evidence
segmentation masks are versioned artifacts and never hidden workflow state
repair and color operations declare their masks, prompts, palette roles and parent revisions
loop history is append-only and reconstructable from attempt receipts
ComfyUI queue retries do not increment semantic repair attempts unless new pixels are produced
accepted and rejected are terminal; debt resumes only by starting a new loop from its declared recovery phase

comfyExecutionPolicy.serviceTopology: one governed ComfyUI service endpoint per worker deployment; multiple versioned workflow jobs share its queue
comfyExecutionPolicy.stageIsolation: generation, inspection, segmentation and masked repair are separate workflow revisions with typed inputs and outputs
comfyExecutionPolicy.processScaling: additional ComfyUI worker processes are a deployment scaling decision and never change domain artifacts or stage semantics
comfyExecutionPolicy.historyPolicy: ComfyUI queue and history are ephemeral execution evidence, not revision or acceptance truth

p0DependencyDecision.ImpactPackAndSubpack: preferred candidate for detector, SAM mask and targeted detail repair; must be commit, hash, node and license pinned before approval
p0DependencyDecision.SAM: preferred candidate for subject mask generation; model artifact must be hash and license pinned
p0DependencyDecision.LayerStyle: optional composition tooling only; not required by the canonical P0 runtime pipeline

acceptanceGates: every required slot has exactly one work item and targetVisualSlotId
every provider output identifies approved workflow, model, nodes and execution receipt
every edit has parent revision and mask lineage
vision evidence and deterministic evidence are both present where required
final revision passes semantic role, Style DNA, alpha or background, dimensions, edge, anchor and provenance checks
all required work items are accepted before production set acceptance
only accepted final revisions can enter same-family assembly or runtime binding
playable completion additionally requires real target binding evidence
forbidden: mixed character platform prop background or UI roles generated as one parent sheet
fixed grid cell prompt used as the primary separation mechanism
one provider call presented as a completed production pipeline
one ComfyUI operating-system process required per domain stage
vision output treated as final acceptance
full production set regeneration because one region failed
Style DNA prompt text copied into templates or workflow files as a second truth
accepted same-family sheet assembled from unaccepted source revisions
runtime binding from draft mask repair or temporary ComfyUI output
fallback to the retired composite parent sheet pipeline
terraDefinitionOfDone: remove the retired mixed-role parent-sheet planner, artifacts and layout fields without compatibility readers
implement AssetProductionPlanner and fail-closed validators for every canonical artifact
register separate pinned workflow revisions for generation, inspection, segmentation and masked repair
implement immutable draft, mask, repair, normalized and final revision lineage
implement per-work-item repair loops and required target visual slot coverage
keep same-family assembly optional, deterministic and downstream of acceptance
prove one real asset through draft, inspection, segmentation, repair, normalization, acceptance and real target binding
prove a three-slot production set uses three independent work items with one shared Style DNA and complete binding coverage
prove failed masks, failed repair, missing receipts, stale workflow revisions and mixed-role sheet requests fail closed
status remains designed until independent test and audit evidence exists
